did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper proposes and evaluates BiADATU-Net, a deep learning model based on the Transformer U-Net architecture with deformable attention and bidirectional attention mechanisms, for the task of intelligent skin lesion segmentation in skin cancer images. Experiments were conducted on dermatological datasets (ISIC2016, ISIC2017, ISIC2018, and PH2).

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper is explicitly labeled as an 'ORIGINAL ARTICLE' and presents a new deep learning model (BiADATU-Net) for skin lesion segmentation. It includes original methods, experiments on four datasets (ISIC2016, ISIC2017, ISIC2018, PH2), ablation studies, and performance comparisons, which are characteristic of original research rather than a review.

title: Intelligent skin lesion segmentation using deformable attention Transformer U-Net with bidirectional attention mechanism in skin cancer images
brief_summary: This study proposes BiADATU-Net, a precision-driven segmentation model for skin cancer images that integrates a deformable attention Transformer and bidirectional attention blocks into a U-Net architecture. The encoder utilizes a deformable attention Transformer with a dual attention block to adaptively learn global and local features, while the decoder employs scSE attention modules in skip connections for strong feature fusion. The model was evaluated on four dermatological datasets: ISIC2016, ISIC2017, ISIC2018, and PH2. Experimental results demonstrated that the proposed model achieved an accuracy rate of over 96% across all datasets. The findings indicate that BiADATU-Net achieves competitive performance supremacy compared to state-of-the-art methods, validating its potential for intelligent skin lesion segmentation.

lesion_types_studied.details: The study focuses on the segmentation of skin lesions in general across four datasets (ISIC2016, ISIC2017, ISIC2018, PH2). While the introduction highlights malignant melanoma as a primary clinical concern and motivation, the experiments treat the lesion as a binary segmentation target (lesion vs. background) without distinguishing specific histological subtypes in the reported results.

Mentioned in the Introduction and Abstract as a major public health concern and the primary motivation for developing accurate segmentation techniques. It is not explicitly listed as a distinct class label in the experimental segmentation results, which focus on general lesion segmentation.

all_task_types.target: Skin lesion masks (binary segmentation of lesion vs. background)
all_task_types.details: The paper proposes BiADATU-Net for 'intelligent skin lesion segmentation'. The model predicts pixel-wise masks for skin lesions in dermoscopic images. Performance is evaluated using segmentation metrics including Accuracy (Acc), Sensitivity (Sen), Precision (Pre), Dice Coefficient (DC), and Jaccard Index (JA) on datasets ISIC2016, ISIC2017, ISIC2018, and PH2.

datasets_used.name: ISIC2016
datasets_used.description: A dataset sourced from the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC) containing pigment lesion areas from different populations, annotated by experts for lesion contour and type.

use.training: Used for training the BiADATU-Net model.
use.testing: Used for testing the segmentation performance.

datasets_used.description: A dataset sourced from the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC) containing pigment lesion areas from different populations, annotated by experts for lesion contour and type.

use.validation: Used for validation during the training process.
use.testing: Used for testing the segmentation performance.

datasets_used.name: ISIC2018
datasets_used.description: A dataset sourced from the International Skin Imaging Collaboration (ISIC) containing pigment lesion areas from different populations, annotated by experts for lesion contour and type.

use.training: Used for training the BiADATU-Net model.
use.validation: Used for validation during the training process.
use.testing: Used for testing the segmentation performance.

datasets_used.name: PH2
datasets_used.description: A dedicated dermoscopy image database comprised of 200 skin lesion images with manually annotated segmentation by dermatologists, designed for clinical research and benchmark testing.

use.testing: Used as an additional test set to evaluate the segmentation model's generalization ability on the ISIC2018 dataset.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report any formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance comparisons were made using descriptive statistics (Accuracy, Sensitivity, Precision, Dice Coefficient, Jaccard Index) without statistical significance testing.

ISIC2016.notes: The proposed model showed performance improvements over U-Net with increases of 2.98% in Acc, 4.35% in Sen, 2.76% in Pre, 2.11% in DC, and 2.87% in JA.

ISIC2017.notes: The proposed model outperformed DA-TransUNet's scores of 0.9553, 0.8869, and 0.8021 on Acc, DC, and JA respectively.

ISIC2018.notes: The model was trained on this dataset. The proposed model surpassed advanced models such as CPF-Net, FAT-Net, and DA-TransUNet.

PH2.notes: The PH2 dataset was not used for training; it served as a supplementary test set to assess generalization ability using the model trained on ISIC2018. The proposed model achieved higher scores than CPF-Net, FAT-Net, and DA-TransUNet.

Ablation Study (ISIC2018 Test Set).notes: The combination of all three modules (DAT, DAD-block, scSED-block) resulted in the best performance. Compared to the baseline, the Dice coefficient and JA increased by 4.27% and 5.81% respectively.

architecture_used.main_model: BiADATU-Net (Deformable Attention Transformer U-Net with bidirectional attention mechanism)

architecture_details.encoder: Composed of CNN blocks, a DAD-block (Deformable Attention Dual attention block), an embedding layer, and DAT (Deformable Attention Transformer) layers. The DAD-block integrates deformable convolution into a dual attention block and is placed at the front end of the deformable attention Transformer.
architecture_details.skip_connections: Incorporate scSED-blocks (spatial and channel squeeze-and-excitation deformable block) to capture image-specific context information for strong feature fusion.
architecture_details.decoder: Traditional U-Net structure (noted as having room for optimization in future work).

training_details.loss_function: Sum of BCEWithLogitsLoss and Dice metric
training_details.hardware: Intel Xeon Platinum 8350C CPU, NVIDIA GeForce RTX A5000 (24GB) GPU

classification_or_output_head.details: Pixel-wise binary segmentation (lesion vs. background) using a decoder that outputs the final segmentation map.

preprocessing_techniques: Data augmentation: random horizontal flipping, vertical flipping, random rotation, grayscale conversion, and adjustments to image hue
Image resizing: uniformly rescaled to 224 × 224 pixels
Normalization: pixel values mapped to the range of 0 to 1
Hair and artifact removal: explicitly circumvented; the model processes raw images directly without additional noise processing methods

data_augmentation_techniques.details: Applied during training to expand training samples and prevent overfitting.

conclusions: The proposed BiADATU-Net model achieves competitive performance supremacy compared to state-of-the-art methods (such as U-Net, R2U-Net, Attention U-Net, CPF-Net, TransUNet, FAT-Net, and DA-TransUNet) across four dermatological datasets (ISIC2016, ISIC2017, ISIC2018, and PH2).
The model demonstrates satisfactory segmentation performance, achieving an accuracy rate of over 96% across all tested datasets.

The integration of deformable attention Transformer and bidirectional attention modules (DAD-block and scSED-block) effectively enhances the learning capability of skin lesion features, facilitating complex feature extraction at both positional and channel attribute levels.

The model exhibits strong robustness and generalization capabilities, as evidenced by its superior performance on the PH2 dataset, which was used as a supplementary test set without being part of the training process.

Ablation studies confirm that the combination of all designed modules (DAT, DAD-block, and scSED-block) yields the best performance, with the Dice coefficient and Jaccard Index increasing by 4.27% and 5.81% respectively compared to the baseline.
The model shows potential and value in the field of skin lesion segmentation and is adaptable for application to a broader spectrum of medical segmentation tasks.

A limitation of the current model is the increased computational complexity resulting from the DAD-block and scSED-block, which poses a constraint for applications requiring high real-time performance.
The decoder section currently employs a traditional U-Net structure, leaving room for optimization to handle more complex data objects in future research.
Future research will consider integrating diagnosis and recognition of skin disease types to better assist in clinical treatment beyond high-precision segmentation.
important_remarks: The study lacks formal statistical hypothesis testing; performance comparisons rely on descriptive statistics without p-values or confidence intervals.
The model does not perform hair or artifact removal, processing raw images directly, which may limit robustness in clinical settings where such noise is prevalent.
The decoder utilizes a traditional U-Net structure, which the authors acknowledge as a limitation and a target for future optimization to handle complex data objects.
The inclusion of DAD-block and scSED-block increases computational complexity, potentially constraining real-time clinical deployment.
The PH2 dataset was used solely as an external test set for generalization evaluation on a model trained on ISIC2018, rather than for cross-validation or training.
The paper does not report the total number of model parameters, limiting the assessment of model efficiency and complexity relative to the reported computational cost increase.
Data availability is restricted, with datasets available only upon request to the corresponding author, which hinders reproducibility.
